journal_entry.reason: When service is delivered against a customer deposit, the liability (unearned revenue) is converted to earned revenue. The deposit was previously recorded as Deferred income (or Contract liabilities). Upon performance, we debit the liability to reduce it and credit Revenue from rendering of services to recognize the earned income.

output_debit_classifier.reason: The transaction describes the delivery of a service for which a customer had previously paid a $100 deposit. Under IFRS 15, revenue is recognized when (or as) the entity satisfies a performance obligation by transferring a promised service to a customer. At the time of the deposit, the entity recorded a liability (Deferred income or Contract liabilities) because the performance obligation was not yet satisfied. Now that the service has been delivered, the performance obligation is satisfied, and the liability is extinguished while revenue is recognized. The entry debits Deferred income (liability decrease) and credits Revenue from rendering of services (revenue increase) for $100. No tax is mentioned in the transaction, so no tax lines are included. Total debits ($100) equal total credits ($100).
